title: Event attributes
description: Event attributes bind user interactions to ViewModel methods. All are optional; their omission disables the corresponding interaction.

data.name: events
data.summary: Batch-declare multiple event handlers in one object. Alternative to individual `onClick` / `onLongPress` attributes for readability.

data.name: onAppear
data.description: Lifecycle callback when view appears (SwiftUI/Compose only)
data.summary: Fired when the component becomes visible (mount / re-mount). Useful for analytics and lazy data fetching.

data.name: onClick
data.summary: Tap / click. Fires on single tap after gesture recognition (≈0–50 ms). Required on Button for interactivity.

data.name: onDisappear
data.description: Lifecycle callback when view disappears (SwiftUI/Compose only)
data.summary: Fired when the component is unmounted.

data.name: onLongPress
data.description: Long press gesture handler (camelCase) - binding only (@{functionName})
data.summary: Long-press event. Default threshold: iOS 500 ms / Android 500 ms / Web 600 ms.

data.name: onPan
data.summary: Pan gesture (drag) events. Receives a vector `{ dx, dy }` per frame.

data.name: onPinch
data.summary: Pinch gesture (two-finger zoom). Receives a `scale` factor per frame.

data.name: onclick
data.description: Click handler function name (selector-based, lowercase) - string only, no binding

data.name: scripts
data.summary: Inline scripts that run on specific lifecycle events (e.g., `onMount`). Use sparingly — prefer ViewModel event handlers.
